replication_study.hypothesis: Among U.S. federal employees, higher overall job-satisfaction scores are associated with a lower probability of intending to leave one’s current agency for another job within the federal government (negative log-odds for JobSat in a logistic model predicting LeavingAgency).

data_plan.source_type: Cross-sectional survey micro-data (Federal Human Capital Survey)
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Appears to pool the 2006 FHCS core with additional agencies/records (407,789 responses vs. 217,504 analysed in the original paper). No year variable is present; thus we treat the file as an expanded 2006 sample.
data_plan.access_details: Delivered in replication_data folder; no additional permissions required.

qualification.explanation: The file contains the same survey instrument items, coding (1–5 Likert), agency identifiers and derived variables used in the original analysis, permitting an analytic reproduction on an independent draw of employees from the same population.
qualification.similarity_to_original: 1. Variables JobSat, LeavingAgency, SatPay, SatAdvan, PerfCul, Empowerment, RelSup, Relcow, Over40, NonMinority, Agency and interaction term Over40xSatAdvan exactly match the specification reported in Table 2 of the original paper (see original_paper.pdf, pp. 755–756; variables list in post_registration.json ‘independent_variables’). 2. Data originate from the FHCS, identical source as stated in post_registration.json ‘source’.
qualification.deviation_from_original: 1. Replication file contains 407,789 rows, nearly double the 217,504 workers analysed in the original study (post_registration.json ‘sample_size’), indicating either reduced item-non-response filters or inclusion of additional agency samples. 2. Variable Agency is coded with two-letter abbreviations instead of full names used in the original authors’ Stata code (see get_dataset_variable_summary output). 3. The replication dataset lacks variables for LeavingGovernment and detailed age dummies, focusing analysis solely on the LeavingAgency outcome.

data_plan.notes: Roughly 3.7 %–4 % missing on most satisfaction variables; we plan listwise deletion to preserve comparability with the authors’ na.omit() choice (see DAR Pitts (126zz).R). No weights supplied; original article did not weight either.

planned_method.steps:
1) Load the CSV from /app/data into R.
2) Drop rows with any NA on variables in the model.
3) Re-create interaction term Over40xSatAdvan if not already present.
4) Estimate logistic regression of LeavingAgency on JobSat + control set, clustering standard errors by Agency (glm.cluster or equivalent).
5) Extract the coefficient, robust SE, z-statistic and p-value for JobSat.
6) Compute average marginal effect of JobSat (optional, for effect size).
7) Compare sign, magnitude and significance with original (β = –0.444, p < .01).
8) Save results to /app/data/replication_output.txt.
planned_method.models: Logistic regression with cluster-robust (agency-level) standard errors.
planned_method.outcome_variable: LeavingAgency (binary: 1 = intends to leave current agency for another federal position).
planned_method.control_variables: Over40, NonMinority, SatPay, SatAdvan, PerfCul, Empowerment, RelSup, Relcow, Over40xSatAdvan.
planned_method.tools_software: R (≥4.1) with packages: data.table, sandwich, lmtest, clubSandwich or miceadds for glm.cluster, and margins for marginal effects.

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Coefficient of JobSat in log-odds units
planned_estimation_and_test.test: Two-tailed z-test (Wald) using cluster-robust SE; significance threshold α = 0.05.

planned_method.inference_criteria: Replication considered successful if JobSat coefficient is negative and statistically significant at p < 0.05; effect size within ±30 % of –0.444 regarded as comparable, acknowledging larger N.

files.DAR Pitts (126zz).R: Original R script provided by the authors of the replication package. Loads dataset, performs na.omit, runs glm.cluster with identical specification. Needs two edits: (i) setwd replaced by relative path, (ii) read.csv path changed to '/app/data/Estimation Data - Pitts (126zz).csv'.
files.run_replication.R: NEW script we will write that sources the cleaned code, writes results to /app/data, and can be called from Docker CMD.

codebase.notes: glm.cluster is provided by the miceadds package; ensure it is installed. All output files saved under /app/data as per policy.

analysis.instructions: From project root, run: docker build -t fhcs_rep . && docker run --rm -v $(pwd)/data:/app/data fhcs_rep Rscript /app/run_replication.R
analysis.comparison_metrics: 1) Sign of JobSat coefficient; 2) Magnitude difference (absolute and % from –0.444); 3) p-value relative to 0.05 threshold.
